fall_armyworm.symptoms: Small holes in leaves, sawdust-like frass, caterpillars inside whorl
fall_armyworm.treatment: Apply Bt (Bacillus thuringiensis) spray or neem-based pesticide. For severe cases, use emamectin benzoate. Scout fields early morning when larvae are active.
fall_armyworm.prevention: Early planting, intercropping with beans or cowpeas, use push-pull technology with Desmodium and Napier grass

late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked lesions on leaves turning brown/black, white mold on undersides in humid weather, fruit rot
late_blight.treatment: Apply mancozeb or copper-based fungicide every 7 days. Remove and burn affected leaves. Ensure good air circulation.
late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties, avoid overhead irrigation, stake plants, apply preventive copper spray

bacterial_wilt.symptoms: Rapid wilting of entire plant, brown discoloration of stem vascular tissue, sticky bacterial ooze when stem is cut
bacterial_wilt.treatment: No chemical cure. Remove and burn infected plants. Solarize soil. Do not replant same crop for 3+ seasons.
bacterial_wilt.prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds, rotate crops, improve drainage, use resistant varieties

northern_leaf_blight.symptoms: Long elliptical gray-green to tan lesions on leaves, starting from lower leaves
northern_leaf_blight.treatment: Apply mancozeb or azoxystrobin fungicide. Remove heavily infected lower leaves.
northern_leaf_blight.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids, rotate crops, plow under crop residue

rust.symptoms: Small orange-brown pustules on leaves and stems, powdery spores when touched
rust.treatment: Apply propiconazole or tebuconazole fungicide at first sign. Repeat after 14 days if needed.
rust.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid dense planting, remove volunteer plants

powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery coating on leaves, stunted growth, leaf curling
powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur-based fungicide or potassium bicarbonate spray. Neem oil also effective.
powdery_mildew.prevention: Good spacing, avoid overhead watering, plant in full sun

aphid_infestation.symptoms: Clusters of small green/black insects on undersides of leaves, sticky honeydew, curling leaves, sooty mold
aphid_infestation.treatment: Spray with neem oil or insecticidal soap. Introduce ladybugs. For severe cases, use imidacloprid.
aphid_infestation.prevention: Companion planting with marigolds, garlic, or chives. Remove weeds. Use reflective mulch.

soil_management.clay: Improve drainage with organic matter. Add gypsum to break up heavy clay. Avoid working when wet. Good for rice and wheat.
soil_management.loam: Ideal soil. Maintain with regular compost. Good for most crops. Apply mulch to retain moisture.
soil_management.sandy: Add compost and manure to improve water retention. Irrigate frequently in small amounts. Good for root crops like carrots and onions.
soil_management.laterite: Often low in nutrients. Add lime to correct pH. Heavy compost needed. Good for cashew and mango trees.
soil_management.black_cotton: Very fertile but cracks when dry. Add mulch. Excellent for cotton, sorghum, and sunflower.

general_tips.composting: Mix green materials (fresh leaves, kitchen scraps) with brown materials (dry leaves, straw) in 1:3 ratio. Turn every 2 weeks. Ready in 2-3 months.
general_tips.water_management: Water early morning or late evening to reduce evaporation. Drip irrigation saves 50% water compared to flood irrigation.
general_tips.pest_scouting: Check fields twice weekly. Look under leaves and inside plant whorls. Early detection saves 80% of treatment cost.
general_tips.seed_selection: Use certified seeds from registered suppliers. Save seeds only from healthy, high-yielding plants. Treat seeds with fungicide before planting.
general_tips.market_timing: Prices peak when supply is low — typically just before harvest season. Consider staggered planting for continuous supply.
